title: IREN vs. Applied Digital: Which AI Data Center Stock Has an Edge?
text: IREN is increasing its AI cloud capacity to 150,000 graphics processing units (GPUs) with a target of $3.7 billion in AI cloud annualized run-rate (ARR) revenues by the end of 2026. The company has secured substantial funding of approximately $9.3 billion over eight months through various means including customer prepayments. Applied Digital is building data centers with close to 1 gigawatt of power capacity across sites that are in development or being marketed, and holds $16 billion in contracted lease revenues. However, growth depends on future lease signings and power infrastructure development. IREN trades at a forward sales multiple of 6.25X compared to APLD's 13.70X. Year-to-date returns show APLD at 28.2% versus IREN's 25.3%. The analyst concludes that IREN has a clear edge over APLD due to stronger near-term contracts, confirmed funding, and more attractive valuation, while APLD faces greater uncertainty around revenue timing. Both stocks currently carry a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) rating.
